title: Internal Description: The State-Space Description
summary: This page outlines the advantages of state-space techniques, including their generality for nonlinear, time-varying, and MIMO systems, compact matrix notation, suitability for digital simulation, and phase-plane analysis for second-order systems. It notes that state-space analysis is covered in depth in Chapter 10. A drill problem asks students to derive state equations for a series RLC circuit using inductor current and capacitor voltage as state variables.

figures.caption: An RLC circuit with a voltage source x(t), a 1 H inductor, a 1/2 F capacitor, and a 3 Ω resistor, with mesh currents q₁(t) and capacitor charge q₂(t) indicated.
figures.description: The figure is a schematic of a single-loop RLC circuit, so there are no coordinate axes. A voltage source labeled x(t) is on the left, a 1 H inductor is on the top branch, a 1/2 F capacitor is on the right branch with polarity marked + and -, and a 3 Ω resistor is on the bottom branch. An arrow labeled q1(t) indicates the loop current direction across the top branch, and q2(t) labels the capacitor charge. Students should notice the series arrangement of the source, inductor, capacitor, and resistor, along with the indicated current direction and capacitor polarity.
